lesson.title: Rank, Nullspace & Column Space
lesson.body: The column space of a matrix A is the span of its columns, which represents all vectors Ax can equal. The null space (kernel) of A is the set of all solutions to Ax = 0. The rank of A equals the dimension of its column space, which equals the number of pivot positions. The Rank-Nullity Theorem states: rank(A) + nullity(A) = n, where n is the number of columns.

rooms.question: The null space of a matrix A consists of:
steps: Set up the homogeneous system Ax = 0 and solve for x.
answers: All vectors x such that Ax = 0
All vectors in the column space of A
All solutions to Ax = b for some b
rooms.explanation: The null space (kernel) of A is the solution set of the homogeneous equation Ax = 0.

rooms.question: A 4×6 matrix A has rank 3. What is its nullity?
steps: By the Rank-Nullity Theorem: nullity = n - rank = 6 - 3.
rooms.explanation: By the Rank-Nullity Theorem: nullity(A) = n - rank(A) = 6 - 3 = 3.

rooms.question: The column space of A is a subspace of which space?
steps: A has m rows, so Ax \in \mathbb{R}^m for any x.
answers: Rᵐ (where m is the number of rows)
Rⁿ (where n is the number of columns)
Rʳ where r is the rank
rooms.explanation: Each column of A is in Rᵐ (m rows), so Col(A) is a subspace of Rᵐ.

rooms.question: The system Ax = b is consistent if and only if:
steps: b must be reachable as a linear combination of A's columns.
answers: b is in the column space of A
b is in the null space of A
rooms.explanation: Ax = b means b = x₁a₁ + ... + xₙaₙ, a linear combination of A's columns. So Ax = b is consistent iff b ∈ Col(A).

rooms.question: Order the steps to find a basis for the null space of A (row reduce and solve Ax = 0):
items: Row reduce A to RREF to identify pivot and free variables
Express each basic (pivot) variable in terms of free variables
Write x as a linear combination of vectors, one per free variable — these form the null space basis
rooms.explanation: Set up the homogeneous augmented system, row reduce, identify free variables, express basic variables in terms of them, then write the parametric solution.

boss.name: The Rank Specter
boss.taunt: Your column space collapses. Your rank is zero. You are nothing!

questions.question: A 5×5 matrix A has rank 5. What can you conclude?
answers: A is invertible and Ax = b has a unique solution for every b
The column space of A is not all of R⁵
questions.explanation: If a square n×n matrix has rank n, it is full rank, hence invertible, and Ax = b has a unique solution for every b ∈ Rⁿ.

questions.question: Which statement about a matrix A with nullity(A) = 0 is true?
answers: The only solution to Ax = 0 is x = 0
questions.explanation: Nullity 0 means the null space contains only the zero vector, so Ax = 0 has no nontrivial solutions — the columns of A are linearly independent.

questions.question: For an m×n matrix A with m < n, what is the maximum possible rank?
questions.latex: A \text{ is } m \times n,\quad m < n
questions.explanation: Rank equals the number of pivot positions. There can be at most one pivot per row, so rank(A) ≤ min(m, n) = m when m < n.
